_meta.description: AstroPay Figma component node IDs — live-extracted from Figma source
_meta.sync: Manual — re-extract via Figma MCP after every design system publish
_meta.sync_note: 2026-05-04 (re-sync) — Email marketing component library reconciled against live Figma. Two changes detected: (1) Promo email content_sizes shrunk ~169px on XXL/XL/L/M/S (XS unchanged) — likely the community-CTA section was removed from the inline footer area; rerun an M template inspection before the next email build to confirm. (2) Two text_layouts removed: Text Layout=Rappi (950:2427) and Text Layout=Uber (950:2435). Library now exposes 6 text layouts instead of 8 — cobrand-specific text compositions consolidated; use single_text or top_left_text paired with the cobrand header variants. 2026-05-04 (initial) — Email marketing component library added (section 950:1972). New top-level key: email_templates. Twelve component groups: promo_email (6 sizes), headers (11 variants), text_layouts, cobranding_logos (22 partners), illustrations (8 use cases — distinct from in-app newsletter_illustration set 789:1786), ctas, menus, onboarding_cta, content_blocks, onboarding_headers, onboarding_horizontal, footers (3 languages × 2 styles).
_meta.runtime_rule: image_node values below are documentation only — at runtime, ALWAYS resolve image slots via findOne(n => n.name === '#Image') after creating or cloning the template instance. Property keys (Title#xxx, Subheadline#xxx, Gradient/Overlay BOOLEAN toggles) vary per format and must be discovered at runtime via Object.keys(inst.componentProperties).

backgrounds._note: Backgrounds section on Design Components page — reference layers for #Image / solid color / image+gradient setups

image_gradient.label: Image + Gradient
image_gradient.use: Image with brand gradient overlay — fullbg ad starting point

brand_assets._note: Brand identity assets (App Icon / ISO / Logotype / Wordmark). Cross-channel — used by paid media (fullbg layouts), in-app (illustration / promo_cobrand), and email (footers, onboarding headers). Never modify fills inside these instances except for fullbg color overrides documented in PAID-MEDIA-AGENTS.md or for white-on-dark fills in email dark-mode footers.

ad_templates._note: Default = Layout=Design 1. Design 4/5 = fullbg (image fills entire frame, no mockup). 16:9 has no Design 5 — variants are 1, 2, 3, 4, 6.

design_2._note: Replaced 2026-04-30 — new node ID. image_node must be discovered at runtime via findOne(n => n.name === '#Image').

design_5._note: Added 2026-04-30. image_node must be discovered at runtime via findOne(n => n.name === '#Image').

inapp.label: InApp
inapp._note: Lifecycle in-app messages — 6 layouts. Use Promo/Cobrand and Cashback Promo for promo flows; Layout6 added 2026-04-30.

newsletter_illustration.label: Newsletters - Illustration (In-App)
newsletter_illustration._note: IN-APP illustration set — 8 use cases. Used by the in-app `illustration` layout. For EMAIL illustrations, use `email_templates.illustrations` (950:2716) — DO NOT cross-reference. Spin variant added 2026-04-30. 'Astronaunt' is intentionally spelled this way in the source file.

email_templates._note: Email component library — restructured 2026-05-07 (simplification) and renamed 2026-05-07 (semantic naming). All masters now use the `Email / X / Y` namespace for scale. The library is composed of: 1 template frame + N content_blocks stacked + optional hero modal at top + standard footer + scoped illustration set. NEVER reference the in-app `newsletter_illustration` set (789:1786) for email — use `email_templates.illustrations` (950:2716) instead.
_runtime_caveats: Matter font is typically NOT installed in working files. Before any TEXT edit, swap fontName Matter → Inter. Style mapping: Matter SemiBold → Inter 'Semi Bold' (with a space, not 'SemiBold'). Matter Bold → Inter Bold. Matter Medium → Inter Medium. Same rule documented in PAID-MEDIA-AGENTS.md § Plugin API rules.

Pre-swap fonts on instances BEFORE `appendChild()` (not just before text edits). `appendChild` itself fails with 'unloaded font' if a descendant TEXT uses an uninstalled font. Pattern: createInstance → walk findAll(TEXT) → swap fontName → appendChild.
CTA buttons in this library use **DM Sans Bold** (32px) — NOT Matter. DM Sans is typically installed but verify; fallback to 'Inter' Bold if missing.

Email channel uses scoped color tokens — `--gunmetal #1c2b29` (text), `--teal-2025 #00dbbf` (CTA background), `--silver #bdbfb8` (secondary text) — NOT the paid-media / in-app `teal500 #42DECA`. See `BRAND.md § 6.1` and `tokens/brand.json` -> `email_overrides`.

Hero modal composition rule: when using a `hero_modals.layout_X` at the top, the hero already carries an H1 96px. Pair with `block.body_cta` underneath (body + CTA, no H1). Pairing a hero modal with `block.h1_body_cta` produces a duplicate H1.

`block.body_cta` (Block=Body + CTA) has **TWO body paragraphs** by default. When only one paragraph is needed, override the first TEXT node and set `visible = false` on the second. The component does not currently expose a property to hide it.

**Footer disclaimer is currently English boilerplate**, not language-matched. The text in `footer.es_ar` and `footer.pt_br` defaults to 'This email was sent to you by AstroPay...' instead of the FSA1399 disclaimer. Manual override of the disclaimer TEXT node is required for FX-touching sends — inject the BRAND.md § 5 exact text matching the user's market language. **Design system bug to fix in a separate pass.**

Replacing image fills with illustrations: the image slot inside `block.image_h1_body_cta` and `block.h1_image_body_cta` is a `RECTANGLE` with an IMAGE fill (not a swappable instance). To populate with an illustration: (1) `exportAsync({format:'PNG', constraint:{type:'SCALE', value:2}})` on the illustration component, (2) `figma.createImage(bytes)` → image hash, (3) `rectangle.fills = [{type:'IMAGE', imageHash, scaleMode:'FIT'}]`. Inside hero modals, the inner Illustration is an INSTANCE — use `swapComponent(targetUseCase)` instead.

Migration note — components renamed 2026-05-07 from legacy names to `Email / X / Y` scheme. The COMPONENT_SET 950:2162 (was 'Newsletters - Promo email', now 'Email / Template') still exists with content-size variants (XXL/XL/L/M/S/XS at 950:2163/2196/2228/2260/2291/2321), but the simplified library only canonically exposes the XXL-resized instance 1020:1951 — agents should not pick alternate sizes unless explicitly briefed. The following sets were genuinely REMOVED 2026-05-07: `headers` 950:2351, `text_layouts` 950:2426, `cobranding_logos` 950:2470, footer rounded variants (950:1522/1548/1574), menu non-logo-dark variants (950:2915/2923/2928). Any reference to those node IDs will fail.

_naming_convention._note: All Figma masters in section 950:1972 follow `Email / Category / Variant` (slashes create folder hierarchy in the Figma assets panel). Inside COMPONENT_SETs, variants follow `Property=Value` per Figma convention.

categories.Email / Hero: Hero modal at top of email — gradient bg + H1 + illustration
categories.Email / CTA / Inline: Inline CTA inside blocks (COMPONENT_SET with `Alignment=...` variants)
categories.Email / CTA Block: Standalone CTA blocks at sizes S/M/L
categories.Email / Footer: Footer with unsubscribe + disclaimer, by language

simple_template._note: The single canonical email template instance. Composed as a vertical stack of content_blocks inside a 1080×2591 white frame, gap 72px between blocks, padding 62px vertical. The instance is sized down from the XXL master variant (3822px → 2591px). Pick how many content_blocks to instantiate based on message density — 1 block for micro-announcements, 2-3 blocks for standard comms, up to 4 blocks before email weight risks Gmail 102 KB clip.
default_anatomy: optional hero modal (`hero_modals.layout_<N>`) for image-led emails — carries H1

hero_modals._note: Hero modal at top of email — gradient dark teal background + H1 96px + illustration. Distinct from `content_blocks` (which live inside the white content section). Use a hero modal for image-led, attention-grabbing emails (FX rate alerts, promo announcements, milestone celebrations). Currently 3 layouts; user-defined more layouts may be added — extend this list by node ID. The naming `Onboarding - header` (legacy) was misleading: hero modals are NOT onboarding-specific.
hero_modals._anatomy: Each layout is a 1080×1229 COMPONENT with: outer GRADIENT_LINEAR fill, inner Text Layout frame with one big H1 TEXT (Matter Bold 96px), and an Illustration INSTANCE (1× swappable). When using a hero modal, omit any block.h1_* underneath to avoid duplicate H1 — use block.body_cta instead.
hero_modals._swap_illustration_pattern: Inside a hero modal instance, find the inner `Illustration` INSTANCE (by name) and call `swapComponent(targetUseCase)` where targetUseCase is fetched from `illustrations` set below.

content_blocks._note: Modular content blocks for assembling email body sections inside the `simple_template`. Mix freely — pick the smallest set of blocks that delivers the message. When the email already has a hero modal at top, use `body_cta` (no H1) underneath to avoid duplicate H1.

h1_body_cta.use: Headline + paragraph + CTA — text-only block. Default for announcements and lifecycle nudges WITHOUT a hero modal.

body_cta.use: Two body paragraphs + CTA, no headline. Use for follow-up sections after the hero already set context. **Has 2 body paragraphs by default** — override 1st and hide 2nd if only one paragraph is needed.

image_h1_body_cta.use: Image rectangle (850×479) + headline + body + secondary body + CTA — image leads. The image is a RECTANGLE with IMAGE fill — replace with rendered illustration via `figma.createImage(exportedPNG)`.

h1_image_body_cta.use: Headline + image + body + CTA — headline leads, image supports.

cta_inline._note: Inline CTA used INSIDE content blocks. Variant property is `Alignment` (renamed from 'Align to'). CTA visual treatment (DM Sans Bold 32px on `--teal-2025 #00dbbf`, radius 48px) is fixed by the template tokens.

cta_block._note: Standalone CTA blocks (sized S/M/L). NOT a COMPONENT_SET — 3 separate COMPONENTs. Use S for follow-up nudges, M for default activation, L for hero CTA in welcome emails.

illustrations._note: Email-specific illustration COMPONENT_SET — variant property is `Use case`. 8 use cases. Distinct component instances from the in-app `newsletter_illustration` set (789:1786) — DO NOT cross-reference. Typo `Astronaunt` was corrected to `Astronaut` on 2026-05-07.

menus._note: Top-of-email navigation. The simplified library exposes only `logo_dark` (renamed from `menu_logo_dark`). Other menu variants (menu_dark, menu_light, menu_logo_light) were removed on 2026-05-07.

footers._note: Mandatory footer at the bottom of every marketing and lifecycle email. Match language to audience: `en` default, `es_ar` for AR market (ES-AR), `pt_br` for BR market (PT-BR). Carries unsubscribe link and disclaimer placeholder — swap by language. **CAVEAT**: the disclaimer text in all 3 footers currently defaults to English boilerplate, NOT the FSA1399 ES-AR/PT-BR text. For any FX-touching send, manually override the disclaimer TEXT node with BRAND.md § 5 exact text in the matching language.

email_frame_naming_convention._note: Simplified after 2026-05-07 library update — single template, no size suffix, no cobrand convention.
